content.problemStatement:
You are given two non negative integers NNN and MMM (N<M)(N<M)(N<M). A beautiful sequence is defined as a sequence S={X1,X2,…,XK}S = \{X_1, X_2, \dots, X_K\}S={X1​,X2​,…,XK​} of distinct non-negative integers, each at most MMM, that satisfies the following properties:


For all 1≤i≤K1 \leq i \leq K1≤i≤K, we have 0≤Xi≤M0 \leq X_i \leq M0≤Xi​≤M.
For all 1≤i<j≤K1 \leq i < j \leq K1≤i<j≤K, Xi≠XjX_i \neq X_jXi​=Xj​.
For all 1≤i<j≤K1 \leq i < j \leq K1≤i<j≤K, Xi&Xj=NX_i \& X_j = NXi​&Xj​=N, where &\&& denotes the bitwise AND operation.
The length KKK is greater than 111.
Given two non-negative integers NNN and MMM (N<MN < MN<M), your task is to print the longest beautiful sequence. If there are multiple sequences with the maximum possible length, you may output any one of them. If no beautiful sequence exists, output −1-1−1.


Input Format
The first line of input contains a single integer TTT the number of test cases.


The first and only line of each test case consists of two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.



Output Format
For each test case


If there's no beautiful NNN sequence print -1.


Otherwise output a single integer KKK --- the maximum length of such a sequence. On the next line, print any valid sequence of KKK numbers separated by spaces.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated non‑negative integers N and M (N < M).
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:
- If no beautiful sequence of length at least 2 exists, output a single line with -1.
- Otherwise, output an integer K on the first line – the maximum possible length of a beautiful sequence. On the second line output any sequence of K distinct integers (each between 0 and M inclusive) that satisfies the conditions, separated by single spaces.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=3, M=7 the zero bits of N are only bit 2. N|2^2 = 7 ≤ 7, so the maximal beautiful sequence is {3,7} (any order). Its length is 2. For N=1, M=2 no extra number can be added, therefore no sequence of length ≥2 exists and we output -1.

hidden.label: edge case with no extra bits

solution.approach:
For any two numbers X and Y in a beautiful sequence we need X & Y = N.
* Every element must contain all 1‑bits of N, otherwise the AND could never be N. Hence each Xi satisfies (Xi & N) = N, i.e. Xi = N plus some extra bits.
* For a bit position where N has 0, at most one element of the set may have this bit set; otherwise two elements would both contain the bit and their AND would have a 1 there, violating the requirement.
* To maximise the number of elements we give each such free bit to a different element and keep all other free bits unset. The element that has no extra bits is exactly N itself.
* Therefore the optimal set consists of N and, for every bit b where N has 0, the number N | (1<<b) provided it does not exceed M. Each such number uses a distinct free bit, guaranteeing pairwise AND = N.
* If no extra number can be added (i.e., no free bit produces a value ≤ M), the longest possible sequence has length 1, which is not allowed by the statement, so we output -1.
Algorithm per test case:
1. Initialise an empty list extra.
2. For b = 0 … 60 (since 2^60 > 10^18):
   • If the b‑th bit of N is 0, compute cand = N | (1LL<<b).
   • If cand ≤ M, push cand into extra.
3. If extra is empty, print -1.
4. Otherwise print K = extra.size()+1 and the sequence N followed by all numbers in extra (any order).
The loop runs at most 61 iterations, so the solution is O(61) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(61) for the temporary list per test case, i.e., O(1) extra space.
